content: Chemotherapy toxicity monitored using CTCAE (Common Terminology Criteria for Adverse Events) grading 1-5. Hematologic: neutropenia (ANC <1500), febrile neutropenia (fever + ANC <500), thrombocytopenia (platelets <100,000), anemia (Hgb <10). Non-hematologic: neuropathy (sensory/motor), cardiotoxicity (anthracyclines - monitor LVEF), nephrotoxicity (cisplatin - monitor creatinine), hepatotoxicity (elevated transaminases), pulmonary toxicity (bleomycin), ototoxicity (cisplatin). Baseline assessment before each cycle: CBC with differential, comprehensive metabolic panel, performance status. Response assessment: RECIST criteria for solid tumors (complete response, partial response ≥30% decrease, stable disease, progressive disease ≥20% increase); PET response criteria for lymphoma. Long-term monitoring: secondary malignancies (AML/MDS after alkylators), infertility, cardiac dysfunction, cognitive changes ('chemo brain').
